title: thymus chamaedrys var. marginatus
intro: Thymus chamaedrys var. marginatus, also known as Creeping Thyme, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen herb that belongs to the mint family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds. The health benefits of Creeping Thyme include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria and fungi. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or applied topically as a salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
